rulings.Case Title: TREVOR NEADEAU VS. BROADWAY MANAGEMENT COMPANY,LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 4, 2025 Line 1. COMPLIANCE HEARING. The Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement) is CONTINUED TO APRIL 29, 2025, AT 9:00 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT. Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than APRIL 17, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY LEE, ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, VS. PANDA EXPRESS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Notice And Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 4, 2025 Line 2. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Notice And Renewed Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action. Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's unopposed Renewed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is OFF CALENDAR. On February 20, 2025, and by way of its adoption of its tentative ruling from the previous afternoon, the court ordered Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order that includes the full text of the court's tentative ruling denying without prejudice Plaintiff's original motion to dismiss the representative action. More than seven weeks have passed, and Plaintiff has not yet submitted the proposed order. Plaintiff may re-notice its renewed motion after he has complied with the court's February 20, 2025 order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GLEN MCGILLAN ET AL VS. VINCENTE RODRIGUEZ DOING BUSINESS AS OAKLEY DESIGN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 4, 2025 Line 4. DEFENDANT VICENTE RODRIGUEZ DOING BUSINESS AS OAKLEY DESIGN + BUILD & RESTORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Defendant and Cross-complainant Vincente Rodriguez's unopposed motion to strike Atlas Painting Company, Inc.'s Answer to Cross-complaint is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The motion is infirm in several respects. First, Moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the Constantino Declaration, which, buy itself, is grounds to order the motion off calendar. (See L.R.S.F., rule 2.7(b).) (The Court's complete tentative ruling is sent by email to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA DANZEY ET AL VS. ARAVO SOLUTIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Only Plaintiff Kelechi Ajayi Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Majed Dakak
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 4, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFFs JESSICA DANZEY. and KELECHI AJAYI's Motion To Dismiss Only Plaintiff Kelechi Ajayi Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770. Plaintiffs. Jessica Danzey and Kelechi Ajayi's unopposed Motion To Dismiss Only Plaintiff Kelechi Ajayi Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770 is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Kelechi Ajayi only may be dismissed as a plaintiff as an individual and on behalf of all others similarly situated. Plaintiffs must file with the clerk a notice of dismissal on the Judicial Council form and present this order when doing so. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
